claim_status: No-claim benchmark-source observations only; benchmark and vendor documentation is not a Turing benchmark result, competitor comparison, statistical approval, performance claim, or Chrome-class evidence.

sources.title: Speedometer 3.1 methodology
sources.observation: Speedometer is an interaction-driven web-application responsiveness diagnostic with suite-specific harness and workload rules.
sources.run_consequence: Pin the exact suite revision and harness, focused page, viewport, profile, host state, repetitions, and every subtest outcome; missing duration is a failed run, not zero.

sources.title: Speedometer 3.1 run instructions
sources.observation: The official instructions require a clean profile, focused benchmark page, quiet host, and controlled power and thermal conditions.
sources.run_consequence: Record profile isolation, page focus, host noise, power mode, thermal state, and deviations before accepting a suite run.

sources.title: JetStream 3.0 in-depth methodology
sources.observation: JetStream reports per-workload behavior and combines workloads with a geometric mean while distinguishing startup, worst-case, and average behavior.
sources.run_consequence: Retain per-workload output, exact suite version, first-iteration treatment, unsupported-workload classification, and aggregate calculation; do not combine suite versions or silently reduce the denominator.

sources.title: MotionMark methodology
sources.observation: MotionMark warms up, adjusts drawing complexity around a target frame rate, estimates a change point, and uses bootstrap confidence intervals and a geometric mean.
sources.run_consequence: Normalize and record refresh rate, viewport class, orientation, GPU and power state, warmup, repetitions, and the suite's statistical outputs before comparison.

sources.title: Benchmark performance regressions
sources.observation: Chromium treats competitive benchmark regressions as actionable engineering signals and names Speedometer, MotionMark, and JetStream among its benchmark surfaces.
sources.run_consequence: Use regression triage as an engineering control only; preserve Turing's equal-security, failure-denominator, resource-attribution, and independent-review requirements.

sources.title: Chromium Telemetry documentation
sources.observation: Telemetry frames browser performance measurement as automation plus trace and metric collection over story sets, with replay controls available for reducing page drift.
sources.run_consequence: A Turing runner must retain story/workload identity, browser command and profile, trace/artifact hashes, lifecycle state, and replay/local-server controls rather than only a score.

sources.title: Web Platform Tests documentation
sources.observation: WPT provides shared cross-browser test-runner documentation, but browser-in-browser execution does not cover all crash and hang edge cases.
sources.run_consequence: Keep compatibility and performance denominators separate, retain out-of-process crash/hang evidence, and never convert a WPT pass rate alone into a Chrome-class claim.

sources.title: Chrome Speedometer 3.1 and JetStream 3 performance announcement
sources.observation: Google reports dated vendor measurements with named hardware, operating system, and suite versions.
sources.run_consequence: Treat vendor scores as dated context only and retain hardware, OS, suite version, release channel, and disclosure boundaries; they are not Turing results or universal targets.

sources.title: Introduction to Windows Performance Recorder
sources.observation: Windows Performance Recorder uses ETW to capture detailed system and application behavior and resource usage, with WPR/WPA roles and recording profiles that affect what the trace can establish.
sources.run_consequence: Record WPR/WPA or equivalent tool version, selected profile, start/stop commands, host privilege and controls, collection interval, ETL artifact hash, and any trace gaps before using host traces as performance or resource evidence.

sources.title: About Event Tracing
sources.observation: ETW separates controllers, providers, and consumers and supports kernel/application events, real-time consumption, and logged traces; buffer and provider configuration affect what is captured.
sources.run_consequence: Bind every trace to provider/session configuration, buffer and loss indicators, controller/consumer identity, timestamp/clock facts, process and thread attribution, and an explicit missing-event policy; a trace file without capture-integrity review is not a complete sample.

unsupported: No Turing browser benchmark has been run.
No owner-reviewed benchmark readiness or statistics analysis exists.
No competitor comparison, fastest/lower-memory/lower-energy, Chrome-class, or public performance claim exists.
No browser-run trace, raw sample, or claim bundle is supplied by this manifest.
